observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory team to strategically plan the completion of one-time cleaning of PPE in terms of inventory taking of PPE items under the General, SEF and Trust Funds, reconciliation of accounting records with the Inventory Report on PPE, disposition of missing PPE items, and those items with no available record of accountability, and on accounting entries and Disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to complete the procedures in the one-time cleansing at the soonest possible time.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Inventory Team for the results of the one-time cleansing where there is a need to prepare with adjusting journal entries to come-up with the reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality at every reporting period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of the subject properties, in accordance with the afore-cited provisions and with the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties for the prevention or further deterioration of those PPE items, generation of possible additional income from sale, relief of unnecessary accountability and for the provision of additional storage spaces for other purposes that may be available upon disposal of those unserviceable PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and/or the Municipal Engineer to submit justification/explanation on the inclusion of projects to the 20 percent Development Plan, whereby existing issues and concerns have not yet been resolved first, thus resulted to its non-implementation, which proved disadvantageous to its intended beneficiaries as the benefits that goes with these projects have not been delivered to them on the purpose intended; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Development Council to assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects, and act accordingly on the matter, while ensuring to include only those developmental projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01, thus immediate attainment of development goals for its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the MDRRMC:
a. cause the appropriate and thorough evaluation of every project to be funded under the LDRRMF for its immediate implementation in order to contribute greatly to the prevention/mitigation of any disaster-related events that have adverse effects on the lives of its constituents, in accordance with Section 12(c) of RA No. 10121; and
b. prioritize allocation of LDRRMF and use the available savings into projects/programs/activities that are implementation-ready, without any restrictions or prohibitions prior to its inclusion in the LDRRMF budget so that funds allocated thereof would be utilized to the fullest within the timelines set by the MDRRMC, thus contribute to achieving the objectives of setting-up LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Submit to the Audit Team all documents supporting the liquidations of cash advances made so as to ascertain the validity and propriety of the grant and liquidations of each cash advances. Otherwise, appropriate audit action would ensue to protect the interest of the Municipality; and

b. Comply strictly with all the documentary requirements for the grant and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure validity and propriety on all disbursements of government funds.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Local School Board coordinate with the Accounting Office relative to the required submission of the the Quarterly and Annual SEF Utilization Reports for CY 2024, and in succeeding years, to the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, and furnish copies thereof to the Sangguniang Bayan and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, pursuant to Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017, to ensure transparency and accountability in reporting the allocation and utilization of the Special Education Fund to the intended users of information.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with the mandate of 4.C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 and Sections 1 and 3 of the PCW Memorandum Circular 2018-04 to ensure that gender issues present in the locality would be fully addressed with the various GAD-related activities set out of the Fund so as to achieve the GAD goals and outcome;
b. empower the GAD Focal Point System to enhance their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools among others; and
c. require the GAD Focal Point System to monitor the implementation of the GAD Agenda according to the budget allotted for the year to avoid under-utilization of the funds intended for each specific programs and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: a.Strategically plan the application of one-time cleansing of properties by setting a deadline to each procedure on which the concerned units/personnel will follow; b. Instruct the Heads of Accounting and Property Units to complete the reconciliation of the inventory Count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and c.Complete the remaining procedures in application of one-time cleansing of PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund and prepare the necessary Statement of Financial Statement of the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Municipal Accountant already observed the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a.Prioritize the reconciliation and evaluation of available records and secure additional documents within 60 days from receipt thereof to establish the updated status of the balances of the fund transfers particularly those grants with carry over beginning balances; b. Submit a letter of justification indicating the reason/s of non-utilization of fund transfers totaling P3,512,740.00 within 15 days from receipt hereof; c. Coordinate with the implementing units to assess the needs for those unexpended fund balances from NGA’s. Should the funds be proven no longer needed, remit immediately to the SA, but if still needed, request authority to use the fund for other related projects of the Municipality; and d. Prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries for the transfer of the grants and donations recognized in the GF book and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to: a. Submit a written justification pertaining to the deficiencies noted in the contract implementation of the Construction of Houses at PAGCOR Village at Sitio Bilog-Bilog, Coral na Munti, Agoncillo, Batangas; b. Require full completion of the project or demand payment of P1,179,309.60 from the contractor, for the cost of items that were uninstalled and not in accordance with the plans and specifications. Otherwise, concerned Municipal officials can be held liable for failing to take the necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the stipulations of the contract agreement; and c. Compute and impose liquidated damages for the unaccomplished/undelivered items considering that the project is not yet 100% complete.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: submit an explanation/justification letter indicating the reasons for the delayed implementation of the projects, within 15 days from receipt hereof. With regard to the payment for the construction of Multi-Purpose/Evacuation Center, we recommended the imposition of liquidated damages equivalent to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay.

We recommended that the Municipal mayor: a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for property insurance of the Municipality based on its financial capacity; and b. Instruct the General Services Officer to prepare the Property Inventory Form and cause the appraisal of the properties, and submit them for application of insurance with GSIS to protect the Management thru indemnification in cases of damage or losses due to fortuitous events.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: a. Require the Collecting Officers to turn-over their cash and collections daily and regularly to the Municipal Treasurer to preclude the misuse of cash in their possession; and b. Require the Liquidating Officer to ascertain that all collections and accountable forms issued to each Collecting Officer must be reflected in the Report of Collection and Deposit in each liquidation made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, to require the Head of the HRMO to: a. Come up with concrete policy guidelines relative to the hiring/rehiring of JO personnel to establish a clear expectation and protect the rights of both the employees and the Municipality; and b. Submit the Agency’s action plan towards the implementation of the foregoing observations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and adopt the procedural guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality; and b. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties with acquisition cost of P5,383,230.61 to prevent further deterioration of the asset and decongest the storage area, in accordance with existing rules and regulations and in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. The Municipal Assessor to declare for tax assessment on the noted lots without Tax Declarations and to provide the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office complete and necessary information of the land owned by the Municipality; b. The Municipality Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after the conduct of comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality; c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain a record of each land by using the Real Property Ledger Card, which shall include a complete description of the lot as to area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, Location and other applicable data; d. The Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain Property Cards for each land owned by the Municipality containing details such as land area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location, actual utilization of such a lot and other applicable data of each lot; and e. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO to follow the guidelines and procedure in the conduct of physical count of land, recognition of land items found at station and disposition of non-existing/missing land items stated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adopt the procedural guidelines provided in COA Circular No.2020-006 to complete the conduct of physical count of all PPE and require the Inventory Committee to perform the required reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each group of PPEs indicating every detail of the properties pursuant to Section 269, Volume 1 of GAM for LGUs; and b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to identify the details of the PPE without specific description along with securing documents pertaining thereto. If it cannot be identified despite all the efforts exerted, disclose those PPEs without specific description in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the Subsidiary Ledger and Local Road Network Ledger Card for the road and its component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures; and disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements; and b. Municipal Accountant and designated GSO to secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports and adopt the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; and b. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties with acquisition cost of P5,383,230.61 to prevent further deterioration of the asset and decongest the storage area, in accordance with existing rules and regulations and in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation of records of Accounting and General Services Office is still on-going. Members of the Appraisal and Disposal Committee were formed. Request for disposal of unserviceable properties is still pending. The Management plans to dispose the unserviceable properties once the inventory of all properties is done.
observations.reason: The Management is still on its process of adopting the procedures of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a. Strategically plan the application of one-time cleansing of properties by setting a deadline to each procedure on which the concerned units/personnel will follow;

b. Instruct the Heads of Accounting and Property Units to complete the reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

c. Complete the remaining procedures in application of one-time cleansing of PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund and prepare the necessary Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity under the Trust Fund for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:

a. Submit a written justification pertaining to the deficiencies noted in the contract implementation of the Construction of Houses at PAGCOR Village at Sitio Bilog-Bilog, Coral na Munti, Agoncillo, Batangas;

b. Require full completion of the project or demand payment of P1,179,309.60 from the contractor, for the cost of items that were uninstalled and not in accordance with the plans and specifications. Otherwise, concerned Municipal officials can be held liable for failing to take the necessary measures to ensure that the project is implemented in accordance with the stipulations of the contract agreement; and

c. Compute and impose liquidated damages for the unaccomplished/undelivered items considering that the project is not yet 100% complete.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the concerned officials to submit an explanation/justification letter indicating the reasons for the delayed implementation of the projects.

With regard to the payment for the construction of Multi-Purpose/Evacuation Center, we recommended the imposition of liquidated damages equivalent to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for property insurance of the Municipality based on its financial capacity; and

b. Instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the Property Inventory Form and cause the appraisal of the properties, and submit them for application of insurance with GSIS to protect the Management thru indemnification in cases of damage or losses due to fortuitous events.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

a. Require the Collecting Officers to turn-over their collections daily and regularly to the Municipal Treasurer to preclude the misuse of cash in their possession; and

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly report the collections as income on the date it was actually collected for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, to require the Head of the HRMO to:

a. Come up with concrete policy guidelines relative to the hiring/rehiring of JO personnel to establish a clear expectation and protect the rights of both the employees and the Municipality; and

b. Submit the Agency’s action plan towards the implementation of the foregoing observations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. The Municipal Assessor to declare for tax assessment purposes the noted lots without Tax Declarations and to provide the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office complete and necessary information of the land owned by the Municipality; b. The Municipal Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to recognize unrecorded lots after the conduct of lot comprehensive reconciliation of lands owned by the Municipality; c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain a record of each land by using the Real Property Ledger Card, which shall include a complete description of the lot as to area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location and other applicable data; d. The Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain Property Cards for each land owned by the Municipality containing details such as land area, title number, tax declaration number, PIN, location, actual utilization of such lot and other applicable data of each lot; e. The GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the conduct of physical count of land to determine the actual location of the lot; and f. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO to follow all the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of land, recognition of land items found at station and disposition of non-existing/missing land items stated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: The physical count of all land was conducted by the Municipal Assessor on December 18, 2023. The Municipal Accountant is still waiting for the final valuation from the Appraisal Committee regarding the transfer of all lots in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to unavailability of details for some of the lands acquired by the Municipality, Property Cards containing the complete details were not yet prepared and the Municipality is still waiting for the final report from GSO regarding transfer of all lots.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Health Officer to secure Property Transfer Reports (PTRs) from the source agencies of donations, henceforth, attach all Requisition and Issue Slips and PTRs of all donations in-kind received by the Municipality related to its COVID-19 Vaccination Program; b. Municipal Accountant to record in the books of the Municipality the inventory as of year-end of all donations in-kind with available cost information, in accordance with Paragraph 96 of IPSAS 23; and c. Municipal Accountant to properly recognize the inventory as of year-end of all in-kind donations without available cost information at their fair values at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal, in accordance with Paragraph 97 of IPSAS 23.
observations.managementAction: Copy of the donations received were presented to the Municipal Accounting Office and GSO for the COVID vaccines received in CY 2022. The Municipal Accountant ensures the proper recording of all donations in-kind that will be received by the Municipality. Starting this year 2023, the offices concerned like Municipal Health Office and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office were informed to submit a listing of in-kind donations for proper recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to deduct 10 percent retention money from each progress payment for infrastructure projects up to 50 percent completion of works, if the project is within schedule, otherwise it should be continuously imposed, in compliance with Sections 6.1 and 6.2 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Starting CY 2023, retention money is deducted from Contractor’s billing/claims, whenever necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. BAC and Municipal Accountant to cause the submission of the documents required by Items 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, including the justification/explanation on awarding the contract with Small B PCAB license for further evaluation of the Audit Team within 60 days from receipt thereof; b. Municipal Accountant to check the documentary completeness of requirements prescribed under the circular to ensure compliance with Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before processing payments in order to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursement; and c. BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to establish the regularity of the procurement process and awarding of contract.
observations.managementAction: A justification was submitted to the Audit Team for the award made to a contractor with Small B PCAB license. A checklist of documentary requirements for infrastructure projects and purchase of goods were prepared and attached to every disbursement voucher for easy monitoring and checking for the completeness of documents. BAC is strictly adhering with the requirements of RA No. 9184 for the procurement process and awarding of contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to employ the use of ATM payroll system for the payment of salaries to Job Order employees within three months from receipt thereof to deter possible risks of loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The ATM payroll system is used for the payment of salary for all Job Orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to: a. Prioritize the optimal and timely utilization of 20% Development Fund. b. Submit within 60 days from receipt thereof the plan for the utilization and implementation of the unutilized balance of 20% Development Fund including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: The following projects sourced from the 20% Development Fund were implemented: Concreting/Paving/Evacuation Center in Sta. Cruz implemented on October 6, 2023; Slope Protection at Bagong Sikat Adia Bridge amounting to P1,000,000.00; Rehabilitation/Upgrading of Barangay Road at Sitio Pultero and Sitio Niyogan, Barigon, Agoncillo; Rehabilitation of PNP building amounting to P2,000,000.00 completed on June 25, 2023; Installation of Water System - PAGCOR Road P200,000.00 and Lightning System P200,000.00 - PAGCOR was completed on April 3, 2023; Construction of Balangon Farm-to-Market Road (Hawili Street) was completed on May 22, 2023. PRDP Counterpart amounting to P500,000.00 was suspended and waiting for PRDP NTP while the Improvement of Multi-Purpose Hall/Plaza in the amount of P2,000,000.00 is for posting. Continuing Appropriation Barigon-Bilibinwang Farm-to-Market Road amounting to P10,984,930.00 was started with 15 percent mobilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adopt the procedural guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to complete the conduct of physical count of all PPE and require the Inventory Committee to perform the required reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management already started the adoption of the procedural guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to complete the conduct of physical count of all PPE and required the Inventory Committee to perform the required reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances of Municipality. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 37-39.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is now on-going between the Accounting Department and the Inventory Committee after the completion of the physical inventory taking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20% Development Fund in the succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready, pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.
observations.managementAction: The Concreting of Barigon-Bilibinwang Farm-to-Market Road commenced last March 23, 2023 and implementation is still on-going. The purchase of Lot for the evacuation Center for Sta. Cruz has been reverted in the Supplemental Annual Budget 03-2022 for Concreting of Balangon Farm to Market Road amounting to P602,362.00 and was completed on May 22, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to send written invitations to at least three observers in all stages of the procurement process pursuant to Section 13.1 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and its Secretariats made sure that three observers were invited every time there was a procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the return of the remaining unutilized/unused fund to the source agency and ensure that all subsequent unused balance of funds received from any national government agencies shall be returned immediately after the completion of the project pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: Out of the P47,046.55, an amount of P31,921.11 was already remitted or returned to their respective sources as of December 2023. The unexpended balance of the amount received from the Tourism Promotion Board (TPB) in the amount of P3,191,000.00 was also refunded. The management is coordinating with the concerned departments and agencies to return the said amount.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each group of PPEs indicating every details of the properties pursuant to Section 269, Volume I of GAM for LGUs; and b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to identify the details of PPE without specific description along with the securing documents pertaining thereto. If it cannot be identified, disclose those PPEs without specific description in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already started the preparation of Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each group of PPEs. Items of PPE without specific description remained undisclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements by the Municipal Accountant. The Accounting Office is still waiting for the submission of the details of components of local roads from the Municipal Engineering Office.
observations.reason: With all efforts exhausted, the Municipal Accountant was unable to identify details of PPE without specific description and therefore some items of PPE remained undisclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the Subsidiary Ledger and Local Road Network Ledger Card for the road and its component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures; b. Disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements; and c. Designated General Services Officer to maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component and secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The GSO already updated the Property Cards for all Local Road Network.
observations.reason: The complete descriptions and cost segregation of road components that were completed in prior years are not yet provided to the Municipal Accountant and GSO for proper recording of Local Road Network.

observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee just finished the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE items. Reconciliation of records with Accounting Department is likewise in progress.
The creation of Appraisal and Disposal Committee will be discussed and recommended to the Municipal Mayor in due time for the one-time cleansing of PPEs. Disposal of the unserviceable properties will follow.
observations.reason: Adoption of the procedural guidelines for the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts will follow upon reconciliation between the Inventory Committee and Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the succeeding receipt of funds from various government agencies are appropriately recorded in their corresponding account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant reclassified the funds received from PhilHealth and PAGCOR were reclassified to Due to GOCCs account per Journal Entry No. 300-2022-05-01235 dated May 25, 2022 for GF and TF under JEV Nos. 300-2022-05-01238, 300-2022-05-1239 and 300-2022-05-1242, all dated May 25, 2022 in the Trust Fund (TF) books.

furnish/provide the Audit Team of the one-time report required to be submitted to the
National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the
Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) pursuant to COA Circular 2020-009 and for
transparency and accountability over the in-kind donations entrusted by the donors to
the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Bids and Awards Committee and
Municipal Accountant to submit noted lacking documents to the Audit Team for
further evaluation and provide explanation/justification on the noted inconsistencies
and deficiencies on the supporting documents attached on the Disbursement
Vouchers.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee and Municipal Accountant to ensure that all subsequent procurements are
supported with properly signed documents and provided with complete information
and were in compliance with the documentary requirements required in the existing
rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to take the necessary
inquiry, study and full understanding on the purpose of the funds received by
the Municipality and to diligently secure the Notice of Authority to Debit
Account Issued (NADAI) or other relevant documents of all subsequent funds
that will be credited to the Municipality’s account;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation or Supplemental
Budget was approved by the Sangguniang Bayan before charging all of the
subsequent transactions against the fund;
c. Municipal Treasurer to refrain from issuing checks prior to the approval of
ordinance approving the appropriation/supplemental budget and without
available funds;
d. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
strictly observe the effectivity dates of issuances of guidelines in the
implementation, disbursements and utilization of the funds received from the
national government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that all of the subsequent cash assistance received by the Municipality
from the National Government shall be distributed to the target beneficiaries
within the prescribed period/timeline;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
to ensure the timely submission of all subsequent liquidation reports and its
supporting documents to the source agency within the prescribed
period/timeline; and
c. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Accountant
to immediately submit the noted lacking documents of 21 beneficiaries such
as accomplished Social Amelioration Card, photocopy of the identification
card with specimen signatures of the beneficiaries, barangay certification and
photo of the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the assistance and ensure
the completeness of all the documentary requirements in the subsequent
programs similar in nature.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure
that all succeeding receipt of funds be duly acknowledged by issuing Official Receipt
pursuant to the PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator to submit to the audit team the LDRRMC’s approved
LDRRMFIP and Supplemental Investment Plan approved by the Sangguniang Bayan
covering the COVID-19 related PPAs charged to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust
Fund book.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated GSO to conduct
yearly inventory taking, and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and submit to the
Municipal Accounting Office for reconciliation, and subsequently submit to the
Commission on Audit not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of
the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain the PPE Ledger Card and use in the reconciliation of properties
against the RPCPPE prepared by the GSO.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all efforts to
identify the details of the PPE with lump sum balances and secure the necessary
documents pertaining thereto, if cannot be identified despite of all efforts exerted,
disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the PPE accounts with
corresponding lump sum balances of which specific details cannot be identified by
the Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly
certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due as of
December 31, 2019 and the amounts collectibles for the CY 2020 and the years
thereafter;
b. the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances as of December 31, 2019
of RPT and SET Receivables and their reciprocal accounts with the total
delinquency tax reported by the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary
adjustments particularly on the recorded Allowance for Impairment-Real
Property Tax Receivable in the General Fund; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to set-up RPT and SET Receivables base on the
certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
for the year to be prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and
Provincial Treasurer to reconcile on a quarterly basis or as often as the transaction
occurs, all the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs and adjust
the identified discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers, if warranted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the causes of
discrepancies of intra-agency accounts by re-examining the noted observations by
the Audit Team and effect the necessary adjusting entries. In addition, present a
reconciled intra-agency account, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, in
the subsequent financial statements to be prepared and submitted to the Commission
on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review
the unremitted balances due to GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG and make proper
adjustments/corrections and ensure that the subsequent deductions be remitted properly
to the concerned government agencies pursuant to Section 6(b) of RA No. 8291, Section
18 of Revised IRR of National Health Insurance Act of 2013 (RA 7875 as amended by RA
9241 and 10606) and Section 3 of RA 9679.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Bids and Awards Committee submit justification on the non-conduct of the
mandatory pre-procurement conference for the Construction of New Municipal
Hall at Barangay Poblacion and ensure that pre-procurement conference will be
conducted in all subsequent procurement with approved budget of the contract
(ABC) of more than Five Million Pesos (₱5,000,000.00) pursuant to the
2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184;
b. the Municipal Engineer submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the
approved plan/drawing for the Construction of New Municipal Hall at Barangay
Poblacion, and the latter submit to the Commission on Audit; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting the request of the contractor on
the advance payment without submission of the letter of credit, bank guarantee
or surety bond as required in Section 4.2 of the Annex E of 2016 Revised IRR of
RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee
to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant all of the lacking documentary
requirements on procurement and the latter to ensure that the authenticated copy of the
approved Annual Procurement Plan, certification from the Head of the Bid and Awards
Committee Secretariat on posting of advertisement at conspicuous places, Construction
Safety and Health Program approved by Department of Labor and Employment and
Program Evaluation and Review Technique/Critical Path Method (PERT/CPM) were
attached on the contract and disbursement voucher before processing for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal
Mayor revisit, re-evaluate and reassess the projects in the Continuing Appropriations
so as to align to the current needs of the constituents and ensure optimal utilization
of all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) funded out of 20 percent Development
Fund pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare an accurate Report
on the Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and consistent
with the other records of the Municipality, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS Executive Committee
and Technical Working Group to ensure that the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget
for the ensuing year had a Certificate of Endorsement from the Department of Interior
and Local Government pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as
amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and the GAD Focal Point System and its Technical Working
Group to establish and maintain an operational GAD Database pursuant to PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System
Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to conduct the gender analysis
on the programs, projects and activities of the Municipality using the Harmonized
Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) Tool pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GFPS to prepare GAD
Accomplishment Report based on the approved GAD Plan and Budget and ensure
consistency of both reports pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a) the General Service Officer to –

o conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment
o accomplish the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) in the prescribed NGAS format
o submit the RPCPPE to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of
each year;

b) the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to reconcile the
differences noted between the Inventory Report and Accounting records in
order not to compromise the reliability of submitted reports; draw the
necessary adjusting entries to effect the reconciliation.

c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPE Ledger Card, Real
Property Ledger Card and Subsidiary Ledger for each category of assets and
reconcile with the balance presented in the financial statements;

d) the Municipal General Services Officer maintain complete and updated set of
Property Cards and to have an updated list of unserviceable items for disposal
with complete details and prepare all the necessary documents/reports to act
on the properties to be disposed within the reasonable time to prevent further
deterioration of the PPE; and

e) the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry appropriately classifying the
erroneously recorded laptop, desktop and its accessories into Information and
Communication Technology Equipment.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the:

a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their respective
records and regularly update their books with the amount debited and credited
to the Cash in Bank account;

b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain separate cash book
and subsidiary ledger to facilitate easy verification and monitoring of the
balances of Cash in Bank account under Trust Fund; and

c) Municipal Treasurer to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the
Municipal Accountant to make proper adjustments in the municipality’s books
of accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a) the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of
taxpayers with name and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the
year at the beginning of the year to facilitate timely set up of the RPT/SET
Receivable;

b) the Municipal Accountant strictly ensure the observance of the existing rules
and regulations on accounting for the RPT/SET Income; and

c) the Municipal Accountant restore in the books of the Municipality the amount
of ₱1,878,654.00 and ₱1,047,741.33 pertaining to RPT Receivable and SET
Receivable, respectively which was unauthorizedly dropped from the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and
Provincial Treasurer to reconcile on a monthly basis or as often as the transaction
occurs all the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs and to
identify the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for
adjustment, if warranted, in order to show the correct balances of the inter-agency
receivables and payables in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant locate and trace the causes of
said unreconciled balances of the intra-agency receivable and payable and effect the
reconciliation of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the perpetual inventory approach in the recording of purchases in
accordance with Sections 114 and 121 of NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
disclose the required reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to
Financial Statements in compliance with paragraph 47 of PPSAS 24 to achieve
transparency in financial reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw
the necessary correcting entries to effect the transfer of interest income earned from
the Trust Fund to the General Fund and to take up journal entry to recognize the
unrecorded interest income for the fourth quarter of CY 2018.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from using Due to
LGUs account in recognizing in the interest income earned from the Trust Fund
account of the Municipality and instead follow the suggested journal entries below in
order to fairly present the yearend financial statements:

Trust Fund Books General Fund Books
Cash in Bank Due from Other Funds
Due to Other Funds Interest Income

In view of this, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
Municipal Officials to implement the projects funded by the National Government
Agencies (NGAs) within the time frame set by both parties to avoid delay in the
delivery of intended benefits that can be derived from such projects. Any unutilized
fund due to some reasons should be communicated to the source agency, otherwise,
any unexpended balance should be returned to the source agencies.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender
equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Executive Committee
and the Technical Working Group:
 adhere to the complete structure and positions of the LGU GFPS provided for in
the MCW IRR;
 ensure the participation of all members of GFPS (Executive Committee and
Technical Working Group) in GAD sensitivity training (GST), gender-
responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis and gender audit in order

to sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and
development;
 see to it that the identified gender issues are formulated from the GAD
Database and forward the database to the province for consolidation.
Assistance of the DILG Operation Field Officer can be sought for as provided
in the Joint Memorandum Circular;
 conduct the early planning and budgeting for GAD so that the GPB can be
forwarded to the PPDO and DILG Regional Office for review and endorsement
within the scheduled timeframe; and
 assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements
of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that
should be in place.

In view of this, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Bids and Awards Committee to:
 observe the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the posting of Notice of
Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the
agency website and PhilGEPS and require the BAC Secretariat to issue
certification on the posting of aforementioned documents in any
conspicuous places in the premises of the procuring entity;
 ensure that the losing bidders will be furnished of the notifications of
Bidding Results indicated in Section 37.1.1 of the revised IRR of RA 9184;
and
 see to it the completeness, authenticity and validity of the bidding
documents and eligibility requirements submitted were duly complied by

the bidders before they are declared eligible.

b. Municipal Engineer, that before signing the Statement of Work Accomplished
strictly ensure that the document was properly accomplished by the contractor
such that the date and actual period of work accomplishment is reflected therein;
and
c. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly
supported with complete documentation required, before making any payment, for
proper control and to provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of
claims.

In view of this, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to:
 carefully plan the PPAs of the LGU in coordination with the MPDC to avoid
unnecessary deferment and delay in the project implementation;
 compute the liquidated damages for the delayed implementation of the project

taking into consideration the days of the invalid suspensions orders and
contract extensions;
 ensure that grants of suspension of work and contract extensions are
compliant with the Sections 9 and 10, Annex E of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184;
and
b. all concerned officials and employees to work hand in hand for the prompt
completion and implementation of the planned projects along side with its proper
documentations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board (MSWMB) to (a) regularly convene to carry out its duties and
responsibilities; and (b) expedite the updating of the Ten-Year Solid Waste
Management Plan as a requirement for the approval of the same by the National Solid
Waste Management Commission and subsequently implement its programs and
activities to promote environmental protection.

We also recommended that the LGU prioritize the establishment of a functional MRF
in every barangay as required by the Section 32 of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PAGIBIG and PhilHealth to identify all
the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the
amount due to these agencies;

b) identify the causes of under and over remittance for some accounts and make
the proper adjustment relative thereto; and

c) explain the difference between the payroll deduction on loan amortization and
the corresponding remittance of the concerned municipal employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to closely
monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings of COA-TAS and
immediately inform the audit team of the courses of actions taken by the contractor.
Otherwise, demand payment from the contractor the amount of ₱255,314.53.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that: a. Municipal Development Council ensure that the Annual Investment Plan constitutes the LGU priority programs, projects and activities to be integrated in the Annual Budget to avoid changes of plan for the effective and efficient use of 20 percent Development Fund; b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to stop the practice of reversion of balances of appropriations and changing the plans under the 20 percent Development Fund without the proper consultation with the Municipal Development Council and the Sangguniang Bayan for their approval; c. Municipal Budget Officer ensure the reversion of funds and passing of Supplemental Budget are supported with the appropriate sources of funds pursuant to Section 322 of RA 7160 and Section 73 of General Appropriations Act; and d. Local Development Council to ensure that the programs and projects under the 20 percent development fund are fully implemented and completed according to plan.
observations.reason: The Municipality has an unutilized balance of 20% and continues to have projects not fully implemented; reversion practices were observed and supplemental budget used as source of funds without proper approvals.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required reports and to strictly adhere with the rules on disclosing information in the notes to financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had not yet submitted the required reports and there was no disclosure made in the notes to financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We againrecommendedthat theMunicipalMayorrequire:
a) the General Service Officer to maintain adequate and updated property
inventory report and cards to recognize the receipts and disposition of
theproperty;
b) the General Service Officer to make an immediate disposal of
unserviceableproperties;
c) the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of
Section 4 (o) of the NGAS Manual for LGUs regarding computation of
depreciation;and
d) the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated and accurate Subsidiary
Ledger for each category of assets and reconcile with the records of the
GSO andbalancepresented inthe financial statements.

We advised the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the
disbursement vouchers and all supporting documents relative to recorded
payables to substantiate that these were legal obligations of the municipality
pursuantto Section 4(s), Chapter2Volume I ofNGAS forLGU.

We also recommended to revert to the Bureau of National Treasury the
amount of ₱679,219.18 as required in Section 122 of Government Accounting
andAuditing Manual (GAAM).

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal

to regularly update the cashbook with the amount debited and

in Bank account balance in the Municipality’s financial statements
atyearend.

Werecommended that Municipal Mayordirect the MunicipalAccountant
and the Municipal Budget Officer to immediately submit the required reports
and to strictly adhere with the rules on disclosing information in the notes to
financialstatements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountant and
the Treasurer to evaluate the long outstanding Due to Other NGAs accounts
amounting to ₱5,834,380.05 and effect appropriate disposition/return to the
source agency or to the National Treasury pursuant to Section 122 of GAAM,
Volume I andSections 4.9and6.7of COACircularNo. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of the
interest income totaling ₱371,853.10 to the General Fund as prescribed in
Section 112 of PD 1445 and Section, of the New GovernmentAccounting System
(NGAS)Manual,Volume 1.

Werecommendedthat:
a. the Municipal Agriculture Officer ensure that the Cattle Fattening
Dispersal be properly monitored and to follow-up payment/refund on
thethree(3) cattle soldby therecipients;
b. the MunicipalAccountant to record and issue JEV for the payment that
wouldmade bythe recipientsof three(3) cattle.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Agriculture
Officer to prepare and submit monthly progress report to COA-LGU and
Department of Agriculture-Regional Office IV-A in compliance with the
MemorandumofAgreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor to check and validate the information indicated in the submitted Tax
Declarations of the seller and cause the necessary action to secure the
corresponding Certificate of Titles from the Land Registration
Authority/Registerof Deeds.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to cause the submission of all the lacking supporting documents to
allow the purchase of lot and to strictly comply with the requirements on the
procurement of land as mandated in Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
and pertinent provision of Presidential Decree No. 1529. Ensure that all claims
against government funds be supported with all the required documentations.
Refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting
documents.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the concerned
officials to strictly follow the Municipal Ten Year Plan on Waste Management in
order to effectively and efficiently implement the program for the benefit of the
constituentsof theMunicipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials
concerned to fully utilize the fund for the implementation of programs/projects
under Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) for the
benefit of constituents particularly in the affected and vulnerable areas and to
refer to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012
and Section 21 of Republic Act 10121 for the recording and reporting of LDRRMF
and useof 30percent and 70percent calamity fund.

We recommended that the Municipality implement immediately
program/projects under ADM projects for the development of its constituents.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the
requirements set forthCOACircularNo.2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the efficient and
effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended
to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes. Likewise, the
Municipality shall implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2013 to publicize the projects/programs/activities or notify the
public on its essential features/elements in the conspicuous places within the
agency premises andin theproject venue.

We also recommended that the Municipality appropriate in its Annual
Budget 20% of their IRA for the 20 percent Development Fund as required in
Section 287oftheLocal Government CodeRepublicAct No. 7160.

We recommended that the LGU allocate 1% of their IRAto established the
Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) as require in Section 15 of
Republic Act 9344 and to organize the Municipal Council for the Protection of
Children (MCPC) and activate, strengthen, and sustain it pursuant to Section 15
ofRepublicAct No.9344orthe “JuvenileJusticeandWelfareAct of2006.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor enforce full
implementation of the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 otherwise known as
the Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to
protectthe environmentandthe healthof theconstituents.

- the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Section 272 of
RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 of the Department of Budget and
Management (DBM), Department of Education (DepEd) and Department of
the Interior and Local Government (DILG) in order to ensure that SEF will
be utilized for the intended purpose and to promote quality education in
every public schools in the Municipality. The non-chargeable expenses are
disallowed in audit and shall be refunded by the officials who allowed the
incurrenceofexpendituresnotincluded in theprioritized expenses.
- the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board to avoid charging
against Special Education Fund the programs, projects and activities that
can be funded of School MOOE pursuant to Department of Education Order
No. 13

Weadvised that the MunicipalAccountant strictly comply with Section 4(6)
of PD 1445. Ensure that all claims against government funds be supported with
all the required documentations. Require submission of lacking documents and
refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting
documents to avoidsuspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the amount of ₱5,300.00 for the overpayment of
wagesberefundedbytheconcern laborers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of the
Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly
those for submission to the audit team with consideration on the preservation
periodof ten (10) years as mandated by law.

 the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjustment on the public infrastructure closed to Government Equity in CY 2016 and prior years in compliance with Philippine Public Sector Standards (PPSAS) for the Local Government Sector and to present a more reliable financial statements.

observations.managementAction: a. all officials and employees ensure that all cash advances did not exceed the prescribed cash advance period and adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, disbursing, utilization and liquidation of previous cash advance; b. the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop granting cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances and issue demand letter to the accountable; c. the Acting Assistant Municipal Treasurer, as the Disbursing Officer, ensure that all officials and employees sign the payroll as a proof that the amount are duly received by the payees; d. the Municipal Accountant exercise utmost diligence in monitoring liquidation of cash advances and check the completeness of the documentations of cash before effecting the liquidation of all cash advances to avoid suspension in audit; and e. the Municipal Accountant use the appropriate accounts in recording cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 for a fair presentation of financial statements.

Municipal Accountant to adjust the double recording of checks totaling ₱5,500.00 by debiting Cash in Bank and crediting the related Expense accounts by the same amount.
observations.reason: Municipal Treasurer has prepared and submitted the Reports of Check Issued daily to the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant adjusted the double recording of checks totaling ₱5,500.00.

observations.managementAction: a. the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan Members to strictly observe the provisions of RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 in the payment of membership fees and annual dues and ensure that payments of fees and annual dues to private organization shall not be charged against the government official funds; b. the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant require the prescribed Official Receipt from the elected officials in relation to the payment of their membership fees and ensure proper documentation; c. the Municipal Budget Officer and the Sangguniang Bayan ensure that the proper procedure in realignment/transfer of funds from one expense class to another is observed before charging the transaction; and d. the Municipal Accountant ensures that the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs provided under the COA Circular 2015-009 shall be used in recording the transactions in the books of accounts and based on the nature of every transaction and not on the item where it is budgeted or charged for a fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: The elected officials have complied with the provisions of RA 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 relative to the payment of various leagues except for the Municipal Vice-Mayor. The Municipal Treasurer requested prescribed official receipts from some elected officials but not all receipts were submitted.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, in their procurement activities, adopt the standard Philippine Bidding Documents, Fourth Edition prescribed by the GPPB and use the standard format of the Contract Agreement provided by the GPPB to ensure that the important provisions were clearly stated and to avoid confusion pursuant to Republic Act 9184.
observations.reason: The BAC has adopted the standard Philippine Bidding Documents and the Municipality has complied by using the standard contract formats in entering into agreements with contractors/suppliers.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and BAC Chairman and its members ensure that all procurements undertaken are in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality pursuant to Section 7 of RA 9184.
observations.reason: The Municipalities infrastructure projects was included in the APP of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. the Municipal Agriculture Officers and LPRAT and Municipal Agriculture Officer to properly orient the beneficiaries of the project and ensure that the cattle fattening project is properly monitored; b. the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record biological assets and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledger per beneficiaries; c. ensure that contracts with beneficiaries are notarized; and d. prepare monthly progress reports.
observations.reason: Poor monitoring by the official concerned on the cattle fattening project as contained in the findings. The Municipal Agriculture Officer failed to furnish the monthly reports initially; other implementation shortcomings remain.

observations.managementAction: a. the Municipal Mayor instructed concerned officers to submit perfected MOA and its related documents to DILG Region IV-A for implementation and compliance with DILG memorandum; b. the Municipal Engineer to compute for the liquidated damages in accordance with the executed contract; c. concerned department heads to provide the necessary action to fully operate the water supply project; d. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the assets and liabilities.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor pursued follow up with DILG on the revised plan on the construction of evacuation center. The Municipal Engineer has not yet computed liquidated damages; the Municipal Accountant had not fully prepared adjusting entries at the time of report.

observations.managementAction: We reiterated that the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 of DBM, DepEd and DILG to ensure that SEF will be utilized for the intended purpose. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board to avoid charging against Special Education Fund the programs, projects and activities that can be funded of School MOOE pursuant to DepEd Order No. 13. The Municipal Accountant to ensure all claims are supported with complete documents and to refrain from processing reimbursements.
observations.reason: Management and DepEd agreed to abide by recommendations; some instances of non-compliance and charges to SEF remain, and reimbursements and supporting documentation issues persist.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Mayor require: a. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation for LDRRMF is not less than five percent of the estimated revenue from regular sources pursuant to Section 21 of RA 10121; b. LDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Plan and annual budget and submit to the local Sanggunian through the Local Development Council and the LDRRMO Plan specifying in detail the list of programs, activities and projects including those charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; c. Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under account Trust Liability-DRRMF in the Trust Fund books, prepare subsidiary ledgers for transfers by year; and d. LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to LDRRMC and Local Development Council to the COA auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant transferred the previous years balance including CY 2017 balance of 70% Mitigation Fund and 30% Quick Response Fund in the Trust Liability DRRM Account 438 and submitted certification showing schedule of balances CY 2014 to CY 2017 and related General Ledger to the audit team. Audit Team reminded Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers by year as required.

observations.managementAction: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor enforce full implementation of the provisions of RA 9003 and: a. modify the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan based on evaluation, ensure submission and approval by National Solid Waste Management Commission; b. assist barangays in establishment of MRFs and ensure Municipality's MRF fully operational; c. prepare the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) for existing open disposal facility and submit to DENR for approval and implementation; and ensure Municipal Solid Waste Management Board set regular schedule of meetings and that the dump site be permanently closed.
observations.reason: MENRO responded that closure and rehabilitation funded by DENR/EMB and MOA entered with DENR-EMB IV-A; 10-year plan is being upgraded and will be presented; MSWMB set regular schedule for 2018; LGU entered MOA with Wisewaste/Bauan Sanitary Landfill for final disposal.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reorganize the GFPS consisting of the Executive Committee (Execom) and Technical Working Group and/or a GAD Secretariat; prepare the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Reports; submit to PPDO and secure approval from DILG; utilize existing database and planning and data-gathering tools to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data until GAD Database is established.
observations.reason: The Municipality reorganized the GFPS and have complied with preparation and submission of the GAD Plan and secured approval from DILG; existing database and planning tools utilized until GAD Database established.
